items.headline: Albemarle Appoints Former Vale CEO Eduardo Bartolomeo as Outside Director
items.snippet: Revenue is projected to be $6.35 billion in 2026, a 23.48% increase year-over-year, and earnings per share (EPS) are expected to turn profitable at $10.18.

items.headline: Albemarle Appoints Eduardo Bartolomeo to Board of Directors
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB) is a world leader in transforming essential resources into critical ingredients for mobility, energy, connectivity and health.

items.headline: Will ALB's Expansion Actions Power Stronger Sales Volumes?
items.snippet: Albemarle saw higher sales volumes (up 14% year over year) in its Energy Storage unit in the first quarter on the strength of its integrated conversion facilities.

items.headline: Albemarle Stock Loses 25% in a Month: What Should Investors Do Now?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation's ALB shares have tumbled 25.5% in the past month, underperforming the Zacks Chemical - Diversified industry and the S&P 500's declines of 6.7% and 0.1%, respectively. Falling lithium market prices are weighing on the ALB stock lately.

items.headline: Albemarle and Cars.com have been highlighted as Zacks Bull and Bear of the Day
items.snippet: Management affirmed its FY26 outlook of $5.7 billion to $6.0 billion in revenue, $2.4 billion to $2.6 billion in adjusted EBITDA, and capex of roughly $550 million to $600 million.

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: Chinese spot prices have climbed back into the $23 per kilogram (kg) range, up from their $10 kg low in the fall of 2024. Analysts project a 4% global lithium supply deficit for 2026.
